response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with broad web, mobile, and embedded experience, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of GPU/accelerator architecture work, SIMD/MIMD experience, DNN training/inference optimization, or chip-level performance analysis. The educational and experiential path does not align with a Principal Performance Architect position focused on AI hardware.
strengths: Long track record of shipping production software across diverse stacks
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang, C/C++ at National Instruments, and embedded work
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, open-source contributions)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No demonstrated deep-learning workload analysis or DNN optimization background
Background is application/web software, not hardware-software co-design or chip architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
